text: A 77-year-old man with metastatic prostate cancer is irritable and asks for morphine exactly at the prescribed times. Most likely explanation?
rationale: Dx: Undertreated pain. Cancer patients often show irritability when pain is not controlled.

text: A 21-year-old man presents with status epilepticus (seizure >5 mins). First-line pharmacotherapy?
rationale: Dx: Status Epilepticus. Tx: Benzodiazepines (e.g. lorazepam) are first-line to terminate activity.

text: A 4-year-old boy has fever, neck stiffness, and trismus (inability to open mouth). Likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Retropharyngeal abscess (RPA). Common in kids aged 2-4 after a URI.
